title: Myrix Mod
description: An exploration based fantasy mod, meant to be used to fill the gaps in bigger modpacks
body:
Hello everyone, and welcome to the **Myrix mod!**

This is a project I've had in the works for a while now, and I believe I'm at the point of doing a first release of the mod. It's something I'm still working on, of course, but I'm happy enough with my progress to publish it, for now.

This mod was mostly made alone, with the help of MCreator and BlockBench. All models, animations and textures were made by me, with one exception: [lukasm0reno](https://www.fiverr.com/lukasm0reno) made the texture for Wyverns.

Now, onto the features of this mod!


<details>
<summary>Swamp Wyverns and Acid</summary>
  
**Swamp Wyverns** are a medium sized predator endemic to...well, swamps. They feed mostly on other animals, hunting them down by **spraying acid** on them, or by simply biting. To a wyvern, a human is just another prey, so be careful! 

![Two Wyverns, roosting in their nest](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a2d78a7cb5d40e93aec21c5e21802a15c2bc94ef.png)

Wyvern acid is a potent substance. While wearing armor will probably protect you, it won't do so for long. In fact the acid is strong enough to **melt through your armor**, taking away decent chunks of its durability

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/NUOnUMW5_eU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Of course, if the acid fully destroys your armor, it will start affecting your health instead.
There are ways to avoid this. The first one is to be lucky. either trough enchanting tables, loot, or villagers, you may stumble upon a new enchantment: **Anti-Acid**, which does exactly what you think. It makes your armor, and by proxy you, immune to acid.

![An iron chestplate being enchanted with Anti-Acid at an anvil](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c9a7d00123b62571dc89c2b1f0f1f057dec4e868.png)

The other, is to be skilled: Slay enough Wyverns, and you'll be able to **craft armor out of their scales**. Wyvern Scale armor is also immune to acid, but is only as protective as iron.

![Wyvern Scale Armor on an armor stand](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/df77214ae0420d77aa3bde5392f61d77af58773c.png)

Alongside their Scales, **wyverns may also drop their guts**. Put those in a brewing stand to get acid potions. Add a touch of gunpowder, and you'll be able to throw acid at your own enemies!

![Acid potions being brewed](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/79e1ede8f60d04d6989137e9ee2fd9e2e7b9647f.png)

V 1.0.1 Onwards: Be brave enough to approach a wyvern, fish in hand, and you may be able to befriend, and even ride the curious reptile!

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/RkmPqJeSz_g" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>
</details>


<details>
<summary>Rats and the Plague</summary>
  
Scattered through the more flat biomes, you can stumble into **Abandoned Farmhouses**.

![An abandoned farmhouse](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/3edcac0fb0ec2bd67dd971d27aad9c0bb773666c.png)

While a good pit stop during your adventure, as they still contain edible food and some tools, these old, decayed buildings do what all abandoned food deposits eventually do: **Attract Rats.**
![Rats inside the farmhouse](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c687da369d9325f71b8d12e401845a58b6cba9de.png)

Why should a seasoned explorer care about a few tiny rodents? Because these rodents **carry the Plague**. And the plague is a **guaranteed Death, with no known cure**. 

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/zdCp5KUOqF4" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

...But if you get rid of the pest, you might get the chance to become a vector of the plague yourself. Very few things can survive the plague...

![A poison potion being turned even deadlier](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/1ba2ef541ec3cc66c5b667b53a075abd238340e4.png)
</details>


<details>
<summary>Kitsunes and Yumis</summary>
As you wander through the Cherry trees, you may stumble into areas thet feel suspiciously flat, and hear a sound like laughter...

![A  small clearing in the middle of a cherry forest](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/337d832adf74200711e3bc24e3189adf8d272b03.png)

...you may even be tempted to walk on them...
<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/xhRrxb0uvLQ" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

...and that's when you'll encounter the small, fox-like predator known as **a Kitsune**!

![A kitsune under a cherry tree](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f1715d4efffb368cd5dd140324851c974886f749.png)

These small predators use their mastery of illusion to bait animals into their den, before munching on their meat...and you are no exception to this behaviour. Win against them, and you might chop off one of their many tails, granting you some illusions of your own. One such illusion is **Illusory Grass**, the very same trick kitsunes used against you. and if you, a human with full mental capacity fell for it, surely so will the mindless monsters that roam the land...

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/hUuXUGnKfL8" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Before we introduce the other main use for kitsune tails, we need to mention a new weapon: the **Yumi Bow**. longer than your usual bow, it has a bigger draw length, and so is much more powerful.

![The player character holding a yumi bow](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8d9fa42c12cda6282c08060bcec51d76dff55243.png)

Tie a kitsune tail to a yumi to get the **Illusory Yumi**

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/w0HZ18T0dd4" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

Notice how it **looks like** the arrow missed, but the husk still took damage? That's the power of illusions.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Ancient Outposts</summary>

V 1.0.3 Onwards:
Interested in the loot of Ancient cities,but too scared, or too unlucky, to visit one? From now on, in areas consumed by sculk, you'll be able to find **Ancient Outposts**. Smaller structures, with the same loot tables as ancient cities, if far fewer chests. But beware, for when the sculk claimed the outposts, it claimed the **knights** guarding them as well.

![An ancient outpost, and its guardian](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0bf3035dc79fab54513fb81e50bb416117297041_0.webp)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Buckler and parrying</summary>
The final standard feature of the mod is a small **Buckler Shield**. It can block attacks like any ordinary shield. However, raise it at just the right time and...

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/meG4WFfuZyQ" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

...you'll parry the mob, knocking them back!
</details>






--------------
The mod also includes larger features, that i'll call **quests**. These are longer, chained together features, that I want you to mostly explore on your own. Adding more quests is what most of the mod's big updates will be about.


<details>
<summary>Quest 1: St.Astrid's Glaive</summary>

Clearly, whatever civilization was there on the overworld before us, they've been to the nether. And because it's the nether, they've surely sent warriors and soldiers into that cursed land. Now, Scattered across the overworld are the Tombs of those warriors, and the churches that held their funerals...

![The tomb of a fallen soldier, resting among the trees](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/31d9ac72fe85818c3ee767a409f460031ae88e85.png)


</details>
<details>
<summary>Quest 2: The Tainted Emperor</summary>

V 1.1.0 Onwards:
We've all seen it. You can't dig too deep in the mountains and caves of this world without stumbling into it. The vile, tainted substance we call Sculk. It is ancient and it is hungry, too dangerous to be left alone. But like all things organic, it too must have a source somewhere. What you'll do one you've found it, is up to you...

![Ancient ruins, defended by a Sculk Knight](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a2fd273fa19b6f65a4f983688a51fd32aa777f30.png)


</details>
--------------

**DEPENDENCIES:** None! 

**RECOMMENDED MODS**: Both The glaives and the yumi bows are fully compatible with **BetterCombat** animations,but they are not required. Also, as I said in the intro, this is meant to be a small piece in a bigger modpack.
**Ice and Fire, ISS**: The rewards for quest 2 are compatible With these two mods
<details>
<summary>Quest 2 Spoilers</summary>

- In the tainting Chamber, you can input I&F Dragon hearts to gain additional tainted  dragon hearts

- In an information table Inputting the cleanser staff and a piece of paper will give you the staff effects as a ISS spell scroll


</details>
 There's plenty of travel necessary, so I suggest finding something to fill in that empty time.
